---
title: "Synthetic Data Comparison: INFO_USE"
subtitle: "TIDES Wave 9 — News & Information Sources"
date: today
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    theme: cosmo
    code-fold: true
    fig-width: 9
    fig-height: 5
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{python}
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "../../")

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
import seaborn as sns
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
from IPython.display import display

INFO_COLS = [
    'INFO_USE_FRIENDFAM','INFO_USE_SOCMED','INFO_USE_SCI','INFO_USE_POL',
    'INFO_USE_CANNEWS','INFO_USE_USNEWS','INFO_USE_INTLNEWS','INFO_USE_LOCALNEWS',
    'INFO_USE_GCWEB','INFO_USE_INDIE','INFO_USE_AI'
]
SHORT = {
    'INFO_USE_FRIENDFAM': 'Friends/Family',
    'INFO_USE_SOCMED':    'Social Media',
    'INFO_USE_SCI':       'Scientists',
    'INFO_USE_POL':       'Politicians',
    'INFO_USE_CANNEWS':   'Canadian News',
    'INFO_USE_USNEWS':    'US News',
    'INFO_USE_INTLNEWS':  'Intl News',
    'INFO_USE_LOCALNEWS': 'Local News',
    'INFO_USE_GCWEB':     'Gov. Websites',
    'INFO_USE_INDIE':     'Indie Media',
    'INFO_USE_AI':        'AI Chatbots',
}
LABELS = {1:'Never', 2:'Once/twice', 3:'Few/week', 4:'Daily', 5:'Multiple/day'}
PALETTE = {'Real': '#2c7bb6', 'IRT': '#d7191c', 'LLM': '#fdae61', 'Agent': '#1a9641'}

def clean(df):
    df = df[INFO_COLS].copy().apply(pd.to_numeric, errors='coerce')
    return df[(df >= 1).all(axis=1) & (df <= 5).all(axis=1)].dropna()

real = clean(pd.read_parquet('synthetic_irt.parquet').pipe(lambda _: pd.read_parquet('../../tides_output/clean_data.parquet')))
irt  = clean(pd.read_parquet('synthetic_irt.parquet'))
llm  = clean(pd.read_parquet('synthetic_llm_qwen.parquet'))
agt  = clean(pd.read_parquet('synthetic_agent_simulation.parquet'))

datasets = {'Real': real, 'IRT': irt, 'LLM': llm, 'Agent': agt}
ns = {k: len(v) for k, v in datasets.items()}
```

## Overview

This report compares real TIDES Wave 9 survey responses on news source usage against three synthetic generation approaches: **IRT** (Item Response Theory), **LLM** (Qwen3.5-9B via Together API), and **Agent-based simulation**.

The question asked respondents how often in the past month they got news from each of 11 sources, on a 1–5 scale (1 = Never, 5 = Multiple times/day).

```{python}
summary = pd.DataFrame({
    f"{k} (n={ns[k]})": [ns[k], v.mean().mean().round(2), v.std().mean().round(2)]
    for k, v in datasets.items()
}, index=['N', 'Grand mean', 'Grand SD'])
display(summary)
```

---

## Mean Responses by Source

```{python}
means = pd.DataFrame({k: df.mean() for k, df in datasets.items()})
means.index = [SHORT[c] for c in means.index]
means = means.sort_values('Real', ascending=True)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
x = np.arange(len(means))
width = 0.2
offsets = [-1.5, -0.5, 0.5, 1.5]

for i, (col, color) in enumerate(PALETTE.items()):
    ax.barh(x + offsets[i]*width, means[col], width, label=col, color=color, alpha=0.85)

ax.set_yticks(x)
ax.set_yticklabels(means.index, fontsize=10)
ax.set_xlabel("Mean response (1=Never … 5=Multiple/day)")
ax.axvline(1, color='grey', linewidth=0.5, linestyle='--')
ax.axvline(5, color='grey', linewidth=0.5, linestyle='--')
ax.legend(loc='lower right')
ax.set_title("Mean news source usage by generator")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
display(means.round(2).style.background_gradient(cmap='Blues', axis=None)
        .set_caption("Mean responses (1–5) by source and generator"))
```

---

## Response Distributions

Distribution of responses (%) for each source, comparing Real vs. synthetic generators.

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(4, 3, figsize=(13, 14), sharey=False)
axes = axes.flatten()

for i, col in enumerate(INFO_COLS):
    ax = axes[i]
    x = np.arange(1, 6)
    width = 0.2
    offsets = [-1.5, -0.5, 0.5, 1.5]
    for j, (name, df) in enumerate(datasets.items()):
        counts = df[col].value_counts(normalize=True).reindex(range(1,6), fill_value=0)*100
        ax.bar(x + offsets[j]*width, counts.values, width, label=name,
               color=list(PALETTE.values())[j], alpha=0.85)
    ax.set_title(SHORT[col], fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_xticks(range(1,6))
    ax.set_xticklabels([LABELS[k] for k in range(1,6)], fontsize=7, rotation=25, ha='right')
    ax.set_ylabel("%" if i % 3 == 0 else "")
    if i == 0:
        ax.legend(fontsize=8)

axes[-1].set_visible(False)
fig.suptitle("Response distributions by source and generator", fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', y=1.01)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Standard Deviations

Higher SD indicates the generator produces meaningful variance across respondents. Real SD is ~1.0–1.5 for all items.

```{python}
stds = pd.DataFrame({k: df.std() for k, df in datasets.items()})
stds.index = [SHORT[c] for c in stds.index]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
stds.plot(kind='bar', ax=ax, color=list(PALETTE.values()), alpha=0.85)
ax.set_xticklabels(stds.index, rotation=35, ha='right', fontsize=9)
ax.set_ylabel("Standard deviation")
ax.set_title("Within-generator variance per item")
ax.axhline(1.0, color='grey', linestyle='--', linewidth=0.8, label='SD=1 reference')
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Correlation Structure

How well does each generator preserve the inter-item correlation matrix of the real data?

```{python}
real_corr = real.rename(columns=SHORT).corr()

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 4, figsize=(16, 4))
for ax, (name, df) in zip(axes, datasets.items()):
    corr = df.rename(columns=SHORT).corr()
    mask = np.zeros_like(corr, dtype=bool)
    mask[np.triu_indices_from(mask)] = True
    sns.heatmap(corr, ax=ax, cmap='RdBu_r', vmin=-1, vmax=1,
                mask=mask, square=True, linewidths=0.3,
                xticklabels=True, yticklabels=True,
                cbar=(name == 'Agent'))
    ax.set_title(name, fontweight='bold')
    ax.tick_params(axis='x', labelsize=6, rotation=45)
    ax.tick_params(axis='y', labelsize=6)

fig.suptitle("Inter-item correlation matrices", fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## K-Means Clustering (k=3)

K-means with k=3 applied independently to each dataset. Cluster centres show the "news diet profile" for each group.

```{python}
np.random.seed(42)
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 4, figsize=(16, 5), sharey=True)

for ax, (name, df) in zip(axes, datasets.items()):
    X = df.values.astype(float)
    km = KMeans(n_clusters=3, random_state=42, n_init=10)
    labels = km.fit_predict(X)
    centers = pd.DataFrame(km.cluster_centers_, columns=[SHORT[c] for c in INFO_COLS])
    sizes = pd.Series(labels).value_counts().sort_index()
    order = centers.mean(axis=1).argsort().values

    cols_short = [SHORT[c] for c in INFO_COLS]
    x = np.arange(len(cols_short))
    colors_c = ['#4575b4','#74add1','#abd9e9']

    for rank, idx in enumerate(order):
        pct = sizes[idx] / len(labels) * 100
        ax.plot(x, centers.loc[idx].values, marker='o', linewidth=2,
                color=colors_c[rank], label=f"C{rank+1} ({pct:.0f}%)")

    ax.set_xticks(x)
    ax.set_xticklabels(cols_short, rotation=45, ha='right', fontsize=7)
    ax.set_ylim(0.5, 5.5)
    ax.set_yticks([1,2,3,4,5])
    ax.set_yticklabels([LABELS[i] for i in [1,2,3,4,5]], fontsize=8)
    ax.set_title(f"{name} (n={ns[name]})", fontweight='bold')
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

axes[0].set_ylabel("Cluster centre (mean response)")
fig.suptitle("K-means cluster profiles (k=3)", fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| output: asis
print("**Cluster interpretation:**\n")
for name, df in datasets.items():
    X = df.values.astype(float)
    km = KMeans(n_clusters=3, random_state=42, n_init=10)
    labels = km.fit_predict(X)
    centers = pd.DataFrame(km.cluster_centers_, columns=[SHORT[c] for c in INFO_COLS])
    sizes = pd.Series(labels).value_counts().sort_index()
    order = centers.mean(axis=1).argsort().values
    print(f"**{name}**\n")
    for rank, idx in enumerate(order):
        pct = sizes[idx] / len(labels) * 100
        row = centers.loc[idx]
        overall = row.mean()
        top2 = ", ".join(f"{k} ({v:.1f})" for k, v in row.nlargest(2).items())
        print(f"- Cluster {rank+1} ({pct:.0f}%): avg={overall:.2f} — highest: {top2}")
    print()
```

---

## Summary

```{python}
#| output: asis
print("""
| Dimension | Real | IRT | LLM | Agent |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Mean range across items | 1.8–3.6 | 1.6–3.6 | ~1.0 | 2.54 (flat) |
| Mean SD across items | ~1.2 | ~0.9 | ~0.01 | 1.31 (uniform) |
| Cluster differentiation | ✅ Rich | ⚠ Thin sample | ❌ Degenerate | ⚠ Intensity only |
| Preserves item ordering | — | Partial | No | No |

**IRT** produces the most realistic item-level ordering but only 11 of 50 personas generated valid (1–5) responses — a generator bug worth fixing.

**LLM** (Qwen3.5-9B) collapsed to near-constant "Never" responses. The persona narratives contain no news consumption cues, so the model defaults to the low end. Adding relevant traits (media habits, political engagement) to personas should improve this substantially.

**Agent** simulation converges to a uniform intensity level, applying the same opinion value to every item. It needs item-specific initial opinions seeded from persona traits to reproduce cross-source variation.
""")
```
